summary: Parallel review: FAIL (4 critical, 5 warning, 15 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 bin/lib/parallel-reviews.mjs:24 — Synthetic crash finding uses fake `review/${role}.mjs:0` path that trips compound-gate's fabricated-refs layer; switch to a sentinel like `🔴 [reviewer-crash:${role}] — …` and whitelist that prefix in compound-gate so fail-closed and shallow-review signals don't conflate

Builder claims verified against code + 581/581 test pass. Implementation is correct: 6-way concurrency confirmed by start-before-resolve test, cwd forwarding tested, fail-closed synthetic 🔴 on `ok:false` covered.

🟡 bin/lib/parallel-reviews.mjs:20 — No `.catch` on the dispatch promise; a rejected dispatch would bypass fail-closed and crash the run. Add `.catch(err => ({ role, ok:false, output: synthetic(err) }))`.
🟡 bin/lib/flows.mjs:0 — Spec AC #2 (synthesis header with totals + per-role count table in `mergeReviewFindings`) is not yet implemented; file as a follow-up task before closing the feature.
🔵 bin/lib/run.mjs:1189 — Consider logging which roles returned 🔴 at completion for faster triage.

🟡 bin/lib/parallel-reviews.mjs:20 — No `.catch` on dispatch promise; a rejecting/throwing dispatch crashes the whole `Promise.all` instead of fail-closing. Real binding always resolves so not exploitable today, but contract advertises fail-closed.
🟡 test/build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:27-54 — Source-regex assertions on run.mjs are brittle (whitespace/rename break them without behavior change). Prefer behavioral tests.
🔵 test/build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:101 — Add a `simplicity` 🟡 case to lock that `[simplicity veto]` is critical-only.

No 🔴 or 🟡. Threat surface is local dev tooling only; no auth/secrets/PII/network boundary touched by this change. Fail-closed synthetic 🔴 on crashed reviewer is correctly implemented and test-covered. Eval written to `.team/features/multi-perspective-code-review/tasks/task-1/eval-security.md`.
